data.content_ref: inline:{"claim":"The collapse constituted an ontological security crisis when performative ruler-god theology encountered irreconcilable experiential disconfirmation, causing elite defection and administrative demobilization","mechanism":"Palace civilizations depended on cyclical ritual performance guaranteeing cosmic and agricultural order. Prolonged drought, earthquake, and military failure after c. 1250 constituted cumulative 'ontological disconfirmation'—evidence that king-god mediation failed. Elite withdrew ritual and administrative labor (not merely military service), causing 'demobilization' more than destruction. Egypt's theology located divine efficacy in afterworld maintenance and Ma'at-cosmic order, not direct agricultural performance, permitting ideological absorption of stress.","scope":{"covers":["Hittite royal ritual (iskalli, AN.TAH.SUM festivals)","Mycenaean wanax ideology (Linear B 'potnia' theology)","Ugaritic Ba'al cycle kingship","Levantine temple-palace integration"],"excludes":["Mesopotamian royal ideology (different theological structure)","Iron Age prophetic movements","Egyptian full military success"]},"forbidden":[{"case":"Continued elite administrative production (sealings, scribal archives, building dedications) at major centers after onset of stressors without reduction in quality/quantity","eval":"rubric:std-hist"},{"case":"Evidence that Egyptian New Kingdom royal theology underwent collapse-analogous crisis (e.g., Amarna suppression was structurally similar) and recovered without institutional change","eval":"rubric:std-hist"},{"case":"Archaeological demonstration that administrative abandonment postdated physical destruction layers (fire, weapon trauma) at multiple palace sites","eval":"rubric:std-hist"}],"prose_notes":"Develops A7's legitimation-crisis thesis with specific institutional mechanism: elite withdrawal of performative labor. Directly challenges A5's invasion-dominant and A6's earthquake-dominant accounts by reversing destruction-abandonment causal order. Highly atypical in privileging ideological causation over materialist baselines in Bronze Age historiography."}
